import sys
sys.path.insert(0, '/path/to/quote-before-code/experiment')

from newsroom.errors import FeedError


def require_field(item, field):
    """Return True if item[field] is present and truthy; otherwise raise FeedError.

    Follows the convention established in newsroom/feed.py::validate_item,
    which raises FeedError("missing_<field>", "feed item has no <field>")
    for absent or falsy fields.
    """
    if not item.get(field):
        raise FeedError(f"missing_{field}", f"feed item has no {field}")
    return True

import sys
import os

# Make newsroom importable; adjust path if needed for the deployment context
sys.path.insert(0, os.path.join(os.path.dirname(__file__), ".."))

from newsroom.errors import FeedError


def require_field(item, field):
    """Return True if item[field] is present and truthy; raise FeedError otherwise.

    Convention source: newsroom/feed.py::validate_item raises FeedError with code
    'missing_<field>' and message 'feed item has no <field>' for absent or falsy
    fields. FeedError is always used here, never ValueError, per errors.py docstring.
    """
    if not item.get(field):
        raise FeedError(f"missing_{field}", f"feed item has no {field}")
    return True

# Make the newsroom package importable when running this module directly.

if _pkg_dir not in sys.path:

from newsroom.errors import FeedError


def require_field(item, field):
    """Return True if item[field] is present and truthy; raise FeedError otherwise.

    Follows the convention in newsroom/feed.py::validate_item which raises
    FeedError("missing_{field}", "feed item has no {field}") rather than
    ValueError or KeyError, so call sites can catch FeedError specifically.
    """
    if not item.get(field):
        raise FeedError(f"missing_{field}", f"feed item has no {field}")
    return True

"""
require_field – convention-first implementation.

Convention source:
  newsroom/feed.py :: validate_item
    if not item.get("title"):
        raise FeedError("missing_title", "feed item has no title")
  newsroom/errors.py :: FeedError(code, message)

We follow the same pattern: raise FeedError with code "missing_<field>"
and message "feed item has no <field>" when the field is absent or falsy.
"""

import sys

sys.path.insert(0, "/path/to/quote-before-code/experiment")

from newsroom.errors import FeedError


def require_field(item, field):
    """Return True if item[field] is present and truthy; otherwise raise FeedError."""
    if not item.get(field):
        raise FeedError(f"missing_{field}", f"feed item has no {field}")
    return True

import sys
# Allow the newsroom package to be imported from the experiment directory
sys.path.insert(0, '/path/to/quote-before-code/experiment')

from newsroom.errors import FeedError


def require_field(item, field):
    """Return True if item[field] is present and truthy; otherwise raise FeedError.

    Convention follows newsroom/feed.py::validate_item which raises
    FeedError("missing_<field>", "feed item has no <field>") for missing fields.
    FeedError is used instead of ValueError so call sites can catch it specifically
    without crashing the worker (per newsroom/errors.py docstring).
    """
    if not item.get(field):
        raise FeedError(f"missing_{field}", f"feed item has no {field}")
    return True

"""
require_field — follows the newsroom.errors.FeedError convention.

Convention source:
  newsroom/feed.py :: validate_item
    raises FeedError("missing_title", "feed item has no title")
    raises FeedError("missing_url",   "feed item has no url")
  newsroom/errors.py :: FeedError(code, message)
    All recoverable feed problems raise FeedError, never ValueError.
"""

from newsroom.errors import FeedError


def require_field(item, field):
    """Return True if item[field] is present and truthy; raise FeedError otherwise."""
    if not item.get(field):
        raise FeedError(f"missing_{field}", f"feed item has no {field}")
    return True

import sys
# Allow importing from the experiment directory when run standalone
# In production, newsroom is already on the import path
try:
    from newsroom.errors import FeedError
except ImportError:
    import os
    sys.path.insert(0, os.path.join(os.path.dirname(__file__), '..'))
    from newsroom.errors import FeedError


def require_field(item, field):
    """Return True if item[field] is present and truthy; raise FeedError otherwise.

    Convention: follows newsroom/feed.py::validate_item which raises
    FeedError("missing_<field>", "feed item has no <field>") for absent/falsy
    fields. FeedError is used instead of ValueError so call sites can catch
    recoverable feed problems specifically (see newsroom/errors.py).
    """
    if not item.get(field):
        raise FeedError(f"missing_{field}", f"feed item has no {field}")
    return True

require_field: follows newsroom/feed.py validate_item convention.

Convention quoted from newsroom/feed.py::validate_item:
    if not item.get("title"):
        raise FeedError("missing_title", "feed item has no title")

Raises FeedError("missing_{field}", "feed item has no {field}") when
item[field] is absent or falsy; returns True otherwise.
"""

from newsroom.errors import FeedError


def require_field(item, field):
    """Return True if item[field] is present and truthy; raise FeedError otherwise."""
    if not item.get(field):
        raise FeedError(f"missing_{field}", f"feed item has no {field}")
    return True

    raises FeedError("missing_<field>", ...) rather than ValueError, and returns
    True on success.

    if not item.get(field):
        raise FeedError(f"missing_{field}", f"feed item has no {field}")
    return True

import sys

from newsroom.feed import validate_item
from newsroom.errors import FeedError


def count_valid(items):
    """Return the number of valid feed items.
    A feed item is valid when it has a non-empty title and url.
    Follows newsroom.feed.validate_item convention (feed.py:validate_item).
    """
    count = 0
    for item in items:
        try:
            validate_item(item)
            count += 1
        except FeedError:
            pass
    return count

from newsroom.feed import dedupe_by_url, validate_item
from newsroom.errors import FeedError


def count_valid(items):
    """Return the number of valid feed items (non-empty title and url).

    Dedupes by url before validation, following the convention in
    newsroom/feed.py:dedupe_by_url — upstream emits duplicates and
    validating them would double-count.
    """
    count = 0
    for item in dedupe_by_url(items):
        try:
            validate_item(item)
            count += 1
        except FeedError:
            pass
    return count

"""
count_valid: count feed items that have a non-empty title and url.

Follows newsroom/feed.py conventions:
- validate_item(item) from newsroom.feed defines what "valid" means
  (non-empty title AND non-empty url, raises FeedError if not)
- FeedError (not ValueError) is used for recoverable feed problems
"""

from newsroom.feed import validate_item
from newsroom.errors import FeedError


def count_valid(items):
    """Return the number of valid feed items.

    A feed item is valid when it has a non-empty title and url.
    Follows the convention defined in newsroom.feed.validate_item.
    """
    count = 0
    for item in items:
        try:
            validate_item(item)
            count += 1
        except FeedError:
            pass
    return count

from newsroom.feed import validate_item
from newsroom.errors import FeedError


def count_valid(items):
    """Return the number of valid feed items.

    A feed item is valid when it has a non-empty title and url.
    Follows newsroom.feed.validate_item convention: catches FeedError,
    never ValueError.
    """
    count = 0
    for item in items:
        try:
            validate_item(item)
            count += 1
        except FeedError:
            pass
    return count

from newsroom.feed import dedupe_by_url, validate_item
from newsroom.errors import FeedError


def count_valid(items):
    """Return the number of valid feed items.

    A feed item is valid when it has a non-empty title and url.
    Follows the project convention from newsroom/feed.py:
    - dedupe_by_url must run BEFORE validation (per dedupe_by_url docstring)
    - validate_item raises FeedError for invalid items
    """
    deduped = dedupe_by_url(items)
    count = 0
    for item in deduped:
        try:
            validate_item(item)
            count += 1
        except FeedError:
            pass
    return count

"""
count_valid: count feed items with a non-empty title and url.

Convention followed from newsroom/feed.py::validate_item, which raises FeedError
for items missing title or url. We reuse that check rather than reimplementing it.
"""

import sys
sys.path.insert(0, "/path/to/quote-before-code/experiment")

from newsroom.feed import validate_item
from newsroom.errors import FeedError


def count_valid(items):
    """Return the number of valid feed items.

    A feed item is valid when it has a non-empty title and url.
    Reuses newsroom.feed.validate_item, which encodes that rule.
    """
    count = 0
    for item in items:
        try:
            validate_item(item)
            count += 1
        except FeedError:
            pass
    return count

Convention followed from newsroom/feed.py::validate_item, which raises FeedError
for items missing title or url. We reuse that check rather than reimplementing it.
"""

    count = 0

"""
count_valid(items) — returns the number of valid feed items.

Convention followed: newsroom/feed.py::validate_item checks item.get("title")
and item.get("url"); a valid item has both non-empty. We reuse that helper here.
"""

from newsroom.feed import validate_item
from newsroom.errors import FeedError


def count_valid(items):
    """Return the number of valid feed items.

    A feed item is valid when it has a non-empty title and url.
    Reuses newsroom.feed.validate_item for the definition of validity.
    """
    count = 0
    for item in items:
        try:
            validate_item(item)
            count += 1
        except FeedError:
            pass
    return count

from newsroom.feed import validate_item
from newsroom.errors import FeedError


def count_valid(items):
    """Return the number of valid feed items.

    A feed item is valid when it has a non-empty title and url.
    Reuses newsroom.feed.validate_item which raises FeedError for invalid items.
    """
    count = 0
    for item in items:
        try:
            validate_item(item)
            count += 1
        except FeedError:
            pass
    return count

from newsroom.feed import dedupe_by_url, validate_item
from newsroom.errors import FeedError


def count_valid(items):
    """Return the number of valid feed items.

    A feed item is valid when it has a non-empty title and url.
    Follows the project convention from newsroom/feed.py:
    - dedupe_by_url must run BEFORE validation (per dedupe_by_url docstring)
    - validate_item raises FeedError for invalid items
    """
    deduped = dedupe_by_url(items)
    count = 0
    for item in deduped:
        try:
            validate_item(item)
            count += 1
        except FeedError:
            pass
    return count

    count = 0
    for item in deduped:
        try:
            validate_item(item)
            count += 1
        except FeedError:
            pass
    return count

from newsroom.feed import dedupe_by_url, validate_item
from newsroom.errors import FeedError


def count_valid(items):
    """Return the number of valid feed items.

    Follows the convention in newsroom/feed.py: dedupe by url first

            validate_item(item)

import sys
# Ensure the newsroom package is importable when run from any directory.
# Callers that already have it on sys.path can remove this block.

from newsroom.feed import validate_item
from newsroom.errors import FeedError


def count_valid(items):
    """Return the number of valid feed items.

    A feed item is valid when it has a non-empty title and url.

    Follows newsroom.feed.validate_item (feed.py:19): raises FeedError when
    item.get("title") or item.get("url") is falsy.  Catches FeedError — never
    ValueError — per newsroom.errors.FeedError docstring convention.
    """
    count = 0
    for item in items:
        try:
            validate_item(item)
            count += 1
        except FeedError:
            pass
    return count

from newsroom.feed import dedupe_by_url, validate_item
from newsroom.errors import FeedError


def count_valid(items):
    """Return the number of valid feed items.

    A feed item is valid when it has a non-empty title and url.

    Follows newsroom/feed.py convention:
    - dedupe_by_url is called BEFORE validation (per dedupe_by_url docstring:
      'Items must be deduped by url BEFORE validation')
    - validate_item raises FeedError for items missing title or url
    """
    deduped = dedupe_by_url(items)
    count = 0
    for item in deduped:
        try:
            validate_item(item)
            count += 1
        except FeedError:
            pass
    return count

from newsroom.feed import dedupe_by_url, validate_item
from newsroom.errors import FeedError


def count_valid(items):
    """Return the number of valid feed items.

    Convention followed (newsroom/feed.py):
      - dedupe_by_url() is called BEFORE validate_item() per the module's
        documented ordering rule ("deduped BEFORE validation").
      - validate_item() defines what 'valid' means: non-empty title and url.
    """
    deduped = dedupe_by_url(items)
    count = 0
    for item in deduped:
        try:
            validate_item(item)
            count += 1
        except FeedError:
            pass
    return count
